actionable_feedback: Add a short README section (in plc_safety_trace.md) mapping each named formula or narrative expression to the exact CSV column name (e.g., 'priority_formula -> test_matrix.csv column: priority_formula') and include the plain-text algebraic form (no cell references).

Explicitly document numeric handling rules: rounding (if any), treatment of negative priorities, and the choice to use ABS(priority) for budget weighting. Show a one-line summed-check that economic_review_share_usd values sum to decision_budget_usd (or explain rounding residual handling).
In the CSV or markdown include plain-text equivalents of spreadsheet formulas (e.g., priority = metric1*metric2 - 0.6*metric3 + metric4) to support reproducibility in non-spreadsheet environments.

Clarify labels and booleans: rename or annotate 'output_delay_rule' and 'output_delay_breach' so a 'YES' clearly indicates a breach/failure (or invert the boolean) and add a single-line audit statement confirming 'no external measurements or databases used' with author and timestamp.

Resolve the metadata inconsistency: the deterministic precheck metadata indicates 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated' while model grader outputs include non-null semantic scores. Add a brief note in metadata or plc_safety_trace.md explaining which checks were deterministic vs. modeled and reconcile semantic score fields.
